post.summary: Trigger a health check on the relay instance (mints a platform JWT and calls relay /health)

get.summary: List all Aegis instances for the tenant

post.description: Returns a one-time `registrationToken`. Configure Aegis with this token so it can complete the registration handshake via POST /cloud-relay/aegis/register.

get.summary: Get an Aegis instance by ID

post.summary: Trigger a health check on the Aegis instance (validates JWKS endpoint)

post.description: Aegis posts its one-time registration token and JWKS URL. On success the instance transitions from `pending` to `active` and the token is invalidated.
post.summary: Complete Aegis instance registration (called by Aegis)

get.summary: Vendor JWKS endpoint (used by relays and Aegis to validate platform JWTs)
List relay connections for the authenticated user's tenant

post.description:
Creates a relay connection. For platform-queue and hybrid delivery modes, an SQS FIFO queue is provisioned automatically (sqs_queue_mode=platform) or the customer-provided queue URL is stored.

The response includes one-time credentials (IAM access key, Aegis registration token) that are never returned again.

tenantId and userId are read from the verified Cognito id_token — no client-supplied headers are trusted.

patch.summary: Update a relay connection (name, URL, linked Aegis)

delete.summary: Delete a relay connection, its SQS queue, and IAM user (if any)

post.description: Makes an authenticated GET to <relay-url>/health and returns latency or error.
post.summary: Test HTTP reachability for a direct or hybrid relay connection

post.description: Sends a canary message to the connection's SQS queue and verifies the send succeeds. Validates that the platform role has sqs:SendMessage permission on the queue.
post.summary: Test SQS queue connectivity for a platform-queue or hybrid relay connection

post.description: slaops-cloud routes the request via the connection delivery_mode. direct: result is returned inline (completed immediately). relay-queue/platform-queue: job is pending — poll GET /cloud-relay/job/:id for the result.

get.description: For relay-queue mode, slaops-cloud syncs status from the relay on each poll. For platform-queue and direct modes, returns the stored job state.
get.summary: Poll for the result of a proxy job

get.description: The relay polls this endpoint to claim jobs for legacy HTTP-polling connections. SQS-enabled relays (local-dev) receive jobs via SQS long-poll instead. Authenticate with the connection api_key as a Bearer token.

200.description: Job claimed — execute and POST result to /cloud-relay/job/:id/result

get.summary: Claim the next pending platform-queue job (called by the relay)

post.description: After executing a claimed job, the relay posts the result here. Set `failed: true` to mark the job as failed.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Job UUID (from GET /cloud-relay/queue/next or SQS message body)

post.summary: Deliver the result of a platform-queue job (called by the relay)

user_id.description: User ID who owns this service
User ID who owns this service

created_at.description: Date when the service was created

updated_at.description: Date when the service was last updated

user_id.description: User ID who owns this service

rawContent.description: Raw YAML/JSON spec content fetched from the URL

bucket.description: S3 bucket where the raw spec is stored

globalOpensearchId.description: OpenSearch document ID of the latest spec in the global catalogue index

operationCount.description: Number of operations in the latest indexed spec

serverCount.description: Number of servers in the latest indexed spec

parameterCount.description: Number of unique parameters in the latest indexed spec

modelCount.description: Number of schema models in the latest indexed spec

lastIndexedAt.description: Timestamp of the last successful indexing run

strategy.description:
manual    — spec is uploaded manually via the portal or pre-signed URL.
url_fetch — platform fetches the spec from fetch_url on a schedule.

url.description: URL the platform fetches the spec from (url_fetch strategy only). Must be publicly accessible or reachable from the SLAOps egress IP range.

cron.description: Cron expression defining the fetch schedule (url_fetch strategy only). Defaults to config[oaspec.url-fetch.default-cron] when not specified.

lastAt.description: Timestamp of the last fetch attempt

lastStatus.description: Outcome of the last fetch attempt

lastError.description: Error message from the last failed fetch attempt

consecutiveFailures.description: Consecutive fetch failure count. When this reaches config[oaspec.url-fetch.backoff-threshold] the scheduler reduces to weekly cadence.

description.example: Stripe REST API for payment processing

externalUrl.description: External URL where the spec can be found or fetched from

managementMode.description:
private  — tenant owns and manages this spec.
platform — tenant has adopted a SLAOps-managed spec from the global catalogue.

description.example: Stripe REST API for payment processing

fetchCron.description: Cron schedule for url_fetch strategy (UTC). Uses platform default when omitted.

fetchUrl.description: URL to fetch the spec from (url_fetch strategy only)

globalOpensearchId.description: OpenSearch document ID of the platform-managed spec in the global catalogue index

name.description: Human-readable name for this relay instance

jwksUrl.description: JWKS endpoint URL of the Aegis instance (must be HTTPS)

name.description: Human-readable name for this Aegis instance

url.description: Base URL of the Aegis instance (must be HTTPS)

jwksUrl.description: JWKS endpoint URL of the Aegis instance (must be HTTPS)

registrationToken.description: One-time registration token issued when the Aegis instance was created

jwksUrl.description: JWKS endpoint URL of the Aegis instance (must be HTTPS)

url.description: Base URL of the relay instance. Used for direct and relay-queue modes. For platform-queue mode, the relay is not called inbound — leave this as the relay identifier URL.

type.description:
managed     — SLAOps-hosted Lambda relay.
self-hosted — Customer-deployed relay on their own infrastructure.
local-dev   — Developer local machine. delivery_mode is locked to platform-queue. An SQS FIFO queue is provisioned automatically.

delivery_mode.description:
direct         — slaops-cloud calls relay synchronously. Relay must be reachable from slaops-cloud.
relay-queue    — slaops-cloud submits to relay queue, polls relay for result. Relay must be reachable from slaops-cloud.
platform-queue — relay polls slaops-cloud outbound and posts results back. Use when relay cannot accept inbound connections.
hybrid         — platform tries direct HTTP first; falls back to platform-queue on failure. Requires both url and sqs_queue_url.

sqs_queue_mode.description: platform — SLAOps provisions and owns the SQS FIFO queue (default for local-dev). relay    — Customer provisions the queue in their own AWS account and grants the SlaOpsSqsPublishRole SendMessage access. Use relay mode when the customer's network cannot reach SQS endpoints in the SLAOps account.

sqs_queue_url.description: SQS FIFO queue URL for this relay connection. platform mode: provisioned by slaops-cloud and stored here. relay mode: provided by the customer at registration time.

sqs_region.description: AWS region of the SQS queue.

aegis_id.description: UUID of the linked AegisInstance. Optional — null means no Aegis for this connection.

iam_user_arn.description: ARN of the IAM user provisioned for relay SQS queue access (sqs_queue_mode=platform only). Null until IAM provisioning is implemented.

iam_access_key_id.description: Access key ID of the IAM credential created for the relay. Stored for operator reference only — the secret access key is never stored.

api_key.description: API key for relay authentication. direct/relay-queue: slaops-cloud sends this as Bearer token to the relay. platform-queue: relay sends this as Bearer token when polling slaops-cloud. Configure the relay with RELAY_API_KEY (inbound) or RELAY_PLATFORM_TOKEN (outbound polling).

type.description:
managed     — SLAOps-hosted Lambda relay.
self-hosted — Customer-deployed relay on their own infrastructure.
local-dev   — Developer local machine. delivery_mode is locked to platform-queue and an SQS FIFO queue is provisioned automatically.

url.description: Base URL of the relay instance. Required for direct and relay-queue modes, and for the HTTP path in hybrid mode. Not used for platform-queue connections (relay makes only outbound connections).

delivery_mode.description:
direct          — slaops-cloud calls relay synchronously.
relay-queue     — slaops-cloud submits to relay queue, polls relay for result.
platform-queue  — relay polls slaops-cloud outbound. Use when relay cannot accept inbound connections.
hybrid          — platform tries direct HTTP first, falls back to SQS on failure. Requires both url and sqs_queue_url.
For local-dev relays this is always platform-queue regardless of what is sent.

sqs_queue_mode.description: platform — SLAOps provisions and owns the SQS FIFO queue. relay    — Customer provisions the queue and grants sqs:SendMessage to the SLAOps platform role; provide relay_sqs_queue_url. Required when delivery_mode is platform-queue or hybrid.

relay_sqs_queue_url.description: Customer-owned SQS FIFO queue URL. Required when sqs_queue_mode=relay. The customer must grant the SlaOpsSqsPublishRole sqs:SendMessage permission on this queue.

aegis_id.description: UUID of an AegisInstance to link to this connection. The Aegis must belong to the same tenant. Null or omitted means no Aegis is linked.

url.description: Base URL of the relay instance. Used for direct and relay-queue modes. For platform-queue mode, the relay is not called inbound — leave this as the relay identifier URL.

type.description:
managed     — SLAOps-hosted Lambda relay.
self-hosted — Customer-deployed relay on their own infrastructure.
local-dev   — Developer local machine. delivery_mode is locked to platform-queue. An SQS FIFO queue is provisioned automatically.

delivery_mode.description:
direct         — slaops-cloud calls relay synchronously. Relay must be reachable from slaops-cloud.
relay-queue    — slaops-cloud submits to relay queue, polls relay for result. Relay must be reachable from slaops-cloud.
platform-queue — relay polls slaops-cloud outbound and posts results back. Use when relay cannot accept inbound connections.
hybrid         — platform tries direct HTTP first; falls back to platform-queue on failure. Requires both url and sqs_queue_url.

sqs_queue_mode.description: platform — SLAOps provisions and owns the SQS FIFO queue (default for local-dev). relay    — Customer provisions the queue in their own AWS account and grants the SlaOpsSqsPublishRole SendMessage access. Use relay mode when the customer's network cannot reach SQS endpoints in the SLAOps account.

sqs_queue_url.description: SQS FIFO queue URL for this relay connection. platform mode: provisioned by slaops-cloud and stored here. relay mode: provided by the customer at registration time.

sqs_region.description: AWS region of the SQS queue.

aegis_id.description: UUID of the linked AegisInstance. Optional — null means no Aegis for this connection.

iam_user_arn.description: ARN of the IAM user provisioned for relay SQS queue access (sqs_queue_mode=platform only). Null until IAM provisioning is implemented.

iam_access_key_id.description: Access key ID of the IAM credential created for the relay. Stored for operator reference only — the secret access key is never stored.

api_key.description: API key for relay authentication. direct/relay-queue: slaops-cloud sends this as Bearer token to the relay. platform-queue: relay sends this as Bearer token when polling slaops-cloud. Configure the relay with RELAY_API_KEY (inbound) or RELAY_PLATFORM_TOKEN (outbound polling).

iam_access_key_id_created.description: IAM access key ID for the provisioned relay IAM user (sqs_queue_mode=platform only). Returned once — not stored. Null until IAM provisioning is implemented.

iam_secret_access_key.description: IAM secret access key for the provisioned relay IAM user (sqs_queue_mode=platform only). Returned once — never stored. Null until IAM provisioning is implemented.

aegis_registration_token.description: One-time Aegis registration token, returned only when a new Aegis instance was registered as part of this connection creation. Never returned again.

name.description: New human-readable name for the connection.

url.description: Updated base URL of the relay. Not applicable for platform-queue connections (no inbound URL).

aegis_id.description: UUID of an AegisInstance to link. Must belong to the same tenant. Pass null to unlink the current Aegis.

connectionId.description: ID of the relay connection that should execute this job

request.description: Full CloudProxyRequestDto payload (HAR request + optional templateContext)
Full CloudProxyRequestDto payload. Stored for platform-queue (relay reads it when claiming) and direct mode. Null for relay-queue (relay owns the request data).

relay_job_id.description: Relay-internal job ID. Set for relay-queue mode only.

result.description: CloudProxyResponseDto or CloudProxyErrorDto. Set once job completes.
